title: <div>  •  “The Philosopher” – Started and engaged in deep class discussions at l
content:
• “The Philosopher” – Started and engaged in deep class discussions at least five times.
• “The Contrarian” – Successfully argued against a class consensus and won.

✔ Neutral Achievements (Behavior-Based)
• “The Lurker” – Never speaks in class, never emails, still gets As.
• “The Apologist” – Used “Sorry for the late email” at least five times.
• “The Editor-in-Chief” – Submitted three or more revised assignments for higher grades.

✔ Negative / Joke Achievements
• “The Excuse Alchemist” – Provided a comically long excuse for late work.
• “Ghost of Discussion Boards” – Never posted a discussion response until the last hour before the deadline.
• “The Bold & The Clueless” – Made a completely wrong analysis in class but spoke with absolute confidence.

✅ Why This Works:
• Tracks participation in a playful way without grading penalties.
• Encourages fun, personal engagement with class mechanics.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 159 (chunk 159/555)
